# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

env:
  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
  PR_DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build and deploy
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "Deploying ${PR_TITLE} to production"
          npm run build
          npm run deploy

      - name: Notify on success
        uses: actions/notify@v1
        with:
          title: "${PR_TITLE} deployed successfully"
          message: "${PR_DESCRIPTION}"

      - name: Notify on failure
        uses: actions/notify@v1
        if: ${{ failure() }}
        with:
          title: "Deployment failed for ${PR_TITLE}"
          message: "${PR_DESCRIPTION}"

      - name: Commit changes
        run: |
          git config --global user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git add .
          git commit -m "${PR_TITLE} - ${GITHUB_SHA}"
